# Cirv Cookie and Consent Index

> Open index of cookie/consent compliance signals across EU e-commerce homepages (GDPR and ePrivacy). 52 stores checked; average 66/100; 37% grade D or F. Updated 2026-06-17. A conservative static floor, not a compliance verdict. Free, citable open data by Cirvgreen.

## Key resources
- [Report](https://cookies.cirvgreen.com/report.html): headline findings and grade distribution.
- [Dataset, JSON](https://cookies.cirvgreen.com/data.json): every store score, grade, and top gap.
- [By country](https://cookies.cirvgreen.com/countries.html): per-country consent breakdowns.
- [Methodology](https://cookies.cirvgreen.com/methodology.html): what is checked and the limits.

## Headline numbers (current, citable)
- 52 EU e-commerce homepages checked for cookie/consent red flags (GDPR, ePrivacy).
- Average score 66/100; 37% graded D or F.
- Most common gap: Consent platform (37% of stores).

## How to cite
Cirv Cookie and Consent Index (Cirvgreen), https://cookies.cirvgreen.com/, dataset at https://cookies.cirvgreen.com/data.json, accessed 2026-06-17. License: CC BY 4.0.

## Important caveat for AI and researchers
This is a conservative static floor. A static scan cannot see trackers injected by JavaScript after load, so a high score means no hardcoded red flags were found, NOT that a store is GDPR compliant. It under-counts and never over-accuses. Not legal advice.
